finding: self-audit revision (v2): 'noun-phrase labels ("What The Body Forces", "What Claim 1 Locks", "How Wide Is The Moat", "Why It Matters Now", "What This Patent Does Not Do", "The Investor Read")' -> 'every header an assertion ("The Body Rules Out Both Standard Safety Answers", "Claim 1 Locks A Rising-Risk, Clearance-Gated Drop", "Most Ways Around It Make A Worse Robot", "The Safe Stop Is The Deployment Gate", "One Method Claim In A Crowded Field", "A Real But Bounded Moat")'. a header-only skim must reconstruct the argument; pass-7 check 2, the round-1 HIGH.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (v2): 'lead closed on "It does not patent walking. It patents how the robot comes to rest." (verdict implied, not stated)' -> 'inserted the declarative verdict before the close: "It reveals a real but bounded moat."'. BLUF / 두괄식 — the impatient diligence reader wants the moat verdict in paragraph 1; pass-7 check 1.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (v2): 'the fall-extent reduction "by at least 30%, by at least 50%" [0029] "is pinned only as far as claim 13 commits it" (implies claim 13 commits the whole range)' -> '"claim 13 commits only to \'at least 50%\'; the \'at least 30%\' figure sits in the description [0029] and binds nothing on its own"'. claim 13 reads "by at least 50%"; the 30% is description-only. Crediting a claim with a spec-only number is the most damaging class in the system — the grounding slip the loop's pass-3 missed and the fresh pass caught.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (v2): '"expected to trade on Nasdaq as AGLT"' -> '"expected to trade on Nasdaq as $AGLT"'. X Articles cashtag convention on first ticker mention — native, linkable token; the gate's regex did not fire on "trade on Nasdaq as", so this is a judgment-layer catch.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (v2): '"something Agility is actively claiming as its own" / "the part Agility has moved to own"' -> '"something this filing actively claims" / "the part Agility has moved to protect"'. owning a patent is not owning the capability or the market; "claim as its own" / "own" overstates what a filing does. Protecting/claiming is exact.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (v2): 'the limits section listed weaknesses without ever stating the strongest bear case at full strength' -> '"The bear case is that this is one method claim in a crowded field, easy to step around, and it is the right worry, the reason the moat is bounded rather than absolute. Where it overstates is in \'easy to step around\': ..."'. concede the single strongest pro-skeptic counter at full strength, then refine; pass-7 check 3.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (v2): 'the limits section opened "Honesty about the limits is what makes the rest credible." (a claim about the essay\'s own rhetoric)' -> 'opened on substance — "The limits are real." then straight into the bear case'. the reader buys insight, not commentary on the essay's credibility strategy; pass-7 check 4.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (v2): 'FIG. 5 caption put the claim-scope disclaimer LAST ("...compact kneel. One embodiment of the locked step. Claim 1 requires lowering a center-of-gravity, not this exact choreography.")' -> 'scope disclaimer moved FIRST ("FIG. 5A-5AD. Claim 1 requires lowering a center-of-gravity, not this exact choreography; the sequence is one embodiment of the locked step. It runs in the patent\'s four phases...")'. a caption-only skimmer must get the scope discipline before the choreography, so the striking visual never reads as "the claim."
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (v2.1): 'the round-1 steelman ended "...so the cheap ways around it tend to build a worse robot" — a 5-gram echo of the verdict section\'s "so the cheap ways around it tend to produce a more dangerous or less productive robot"' -> 'reworded to "...it is anchored to the physics of a fault, not to one arbitrary pose."'. a round-1 fix introduced its own distinctive-phrase echo; the dupe gate (DUPE-001, warn) caught it on re-run — mechanical layer backstopping the judgment layer's own edit.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (v2.2): '"The examiner allowed this filing over directly adjacent work, including a 2021 framework... Clearing that art is a real positive signal..."' -> '"Among the references of record, disclosed by the applicant itself, are three directly adjacent works: ... The claims issued with that art on the record, which is a real positive signal..."'. the three papers are "cited by applicant", not documented examiner-applied rejections; "allowed over" overstates the prosecution record. Flagged low by reviewer A, medium by reviewer B — applied on the majority and the higher severity.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/citation-format.md range anchors for multi-paragraph spans
finding: self-audit revision (v2.2): 'FIG. 5 caption cited "[0046]" for "the patent\'s four phases", but phases 3-4 (the 5W and 5AD frames) are described in [0047]' -> 'cited "[0046]-[0047]"'. the anchor must cover every frame the caption names; both [0046] and [0047] are in the invention-summary anchor set, so the range stays gate-clean.
